title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: Imagine want know genes actively read — translated protein — specific, rare cell type buried inside complex tissue like brain. easily sort cells ; intermingled hundreds cell types. TRAP-seq PhosphoTRAP molecular techniques let tag ribosomes (cellular machinery reads genes) target cells, pull attached RNA. RNA capture immunoprecipitation step — IP fraction — reflects target cells actively translating. keep leftover, un-pulled RNA — INPUT fraction — reference total gene expression tissue. core question : genes enriched IP relative input? gene enriched IP translated target population; gene depleted IP present tissue cells interest. Answering question requires normalising data, picking statistical test, adjusting multiple comparisons across thousands genes, visualising results — pTRAPPING handles three functions. PhosphoTRAP adds one layer: instead constitutively tagging ribosomes, captures ribosomes carry phosphorylated form S6 protein — marker recent neural activation. means can ask just “genes translated cells?” “genes translated cells right now, specific stimulus?”. experimental design otherwise identical: IP fraction (activated cells’ RNA) vs. INPUT fraction (total tissue RNA).

what: The example datasets: Tan et al. (2016)
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: Throughout vignette use data Tan et al. (2016) Cell 167, 47–59, landmark PhosphoTRAP study mice. Dataset 1: Identify warm-sentive neurons Experiment: identify genes highly enriched warm-sensitive neurons, mice challenged heat phosphorilated ribosomes neurons fired response isolated immunoprecipitation. RNA immunoprecipitates sequenced fold-enrichment gene calculated IP/Input fractions. data set RPKM normalized counts (counts per million, scaled TMM-adjusted library sizes, divided gene length kilobases) 8863 genes across 3 biological replicates (3 mice - IP Input sample ). column names follow convention pTRAPPING expects: name contains treatment label (WC - warm-cells), fraction keyword (IP Input), replicate number (1 2), order separated underscores. function parses automatically — metadata table needed.
code: library(pTRAPPING) library(dplyr) library(tibble)  # Pre-computed RPKM values — already normalised; use with norm.method = "none" warm_counts <- read.delim(   system.file("extdata", "warm_counts.txt", package = "pTRAPPING") )  dim(warm_counts) # 8863 genes × 9 columns (1 gene-ID + 8 samples) #> [1] 13724     7 names(warm_counts) # column names encode treatment, fraction, and replicate #> [1] "Gene"      "WC1_INPUT" "WC1_IP"    "WC2_INPUT" "WC2_IP"    "WC3_INPUT" #> [7] "WC3_IP"

title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: ptrap_de() compares IP fraction INPUT fraction given treatment group returns ranked table genes based four available methods. Use table quick guide pick model normalisation method: three GLM-based methods ("LRT", "QLF", "deseq") LM-based "voom" normalise data internally — need normalise first. t-test methods can choose normalisation via norm.method: Note "RPKM": option requires supply gene lengths via gene.length (named numeric vector, base pairs). Without , function throw error. already pre-normalised matrix — Tan et al. dataset — use norm.method = "none" instead. Note filter = FALSE: default, ptrap_de() removes low-expression genes using filterByExpr, expects raw integer counts. passing pre-normalised matrix (RPKM, TPM, similar), small floating-point values mislead filter cause real genes dropped. Set filter = FALSE whenever norm.method = "none" used pre-normalised input.

what: Calculating gene fold enrichment and p-values with the paired t-test
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: use paired t-test — approach original paper. Tan et al. ran test RPKM values, pass pre-normalised RPKM matrix set norm.method = "none" filter = FALSE skip additional normalisation step. Also, pior.count set 0 RPKM values already normalised require pseudocount. function prints message showing auto-parsed column names: treatments, replicate numbers (blocks), fractions. argument “paired.ttest” test_method argument (well “unpaired.ttest”) returns named list including $results $fe tables. Inspect result:
code: warm_de <- warm_counts |>   dplyr::filter(dplyr::if_any(dplyr::where(is.numeric), ~ .x > 1)) |> #keep counts > 1   ptrap_de(     treatment_name = "WC", # only one treatment group in this dataset     test_method = "paired.ttest",     norm.method = "none", # data are pre-normalised     filter = FALSE, # skip count-based filtering     prior.count = 0 # no pseudocount needed for RPKM values   ) # The paired.ttest method always returns a named list names(warm_de) #> [1] "results" "fe"  # $results: one row per gene, sorted by p-value head(warm_de$results) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   Gene     logFC t_statistic    PValue   FDR treatment diffexpressed #>   <chr>    <dbl>       <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> <chr>     <chr>         #> 1 Xpr1    -0.775      -210.  0.0000226 0.178 WC        NO            #> 2 Slain1  -1.98       -152.  0.0000435 0.178 WC        NO            #> 3 Larp6   -0.717      -149.  0.0000453 0.178 WC        NO            #> 4 Zfp187  -0.827      -135.  0.0000548 0.178 WC        NO            #> 5 Gm10032 -0.617       -95.5 0.000110  0.209 WC        NO            #> 6 Wars2   -0.735       -91.9 0.000118  0.209 WC        NO

title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: $results output tibble one row per gene, sorted p-value. columns include log fold change (logFC), p-value, FDR, classification whether gene differentially expressed (DE) based user-defined thresholds logFC FDR. default, function uses logFC threshold 1 (meaning 2-fold change) FDR threshold 0.05 classify genes “” (enriched IP), “” (depleted IP), “” (DE).

title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: diffexpressed column determined two thresholds (must met): lfc_threshold — minimum absolute logFC (default 1, meaning ≥ 2× change) fdr_threshold — maximum FDR (default 0.05) can change without re-running analysis — ptrap_volcano() ptrap_volcano2() recompute classification chosen thresholds.

title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: paired t-test also returns $fe tibble animal’s IP/INPUT fold enrichment (FE) every gene. useful spot-checking individual genes running downstream analyses.

what: Step 2 — Visualise the results with ptrap_volcano()
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: volcano plot puts logFC x-axis statistical significance (-log p-value) y-axis. Genes upper-right corner strongly enriched statistically significant — interesting candidates. Genes upper-left strongly depleted. Grey points fail one thresholds. interactive = FALSE (default), labels always shown genes genes.annot (specified). Volcano plot showing known warm-sensitive markers Tan et al. (2016). interactive = TRUE, plot becomes interactive via Plotly users can zoom hover points see gene labels, logFC, p-value, FDR. Volcano plot showing known warm-sensitive markers Tan et al. (2016). Key arguments:
code: warm_de$results |>   ptrap_volcano(     fdr = FALSE, # use raw p-values (n=3; FDR is very conservative)     log_base = 2, # -log2(p) on y-axis, matching Tan et al. 2016     point_alpha = 0.3,     genes.annot = c(       "Fosl2",       "Egr2",       "Bdnf",       "Adcyap1",       "Junb",       "Fosb",       "Rrad",       "Gadd45b"     ) # known warm-sensitive markers   ) warm_de$results |>   ptrap_volcano(     fdr = FALSE,     log_base = 2, # -log2(p) on y-axis, matching Tan et al. 2016     point_alpha = 0.3,     interactive = TRUE) # hover to show gene labels and info

previous_headings: Step 2 — Visualise the results with ptrap_volcano()
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: Pass named vector override defaults: function returns ggplot2 object, can add override layer standard ggplot2 syntax:
code: warm_de$results |>   ptrap_volcano(     fdr = FALSE,     colors = c("UP" = "#D55E00", "DOWN" = "#56B4E9"),     title = "Volcano plot — custom colours"   ) library(ggplot2)  warm_de$results |>   ptrap_volcano(fdr = FALSE) +   theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) +   xlim(-10, 10)

what: Step 3 — Compare two conditions with ptrap_volcano2()
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: Dataset 2: Characterising warm-sensitive neurons Experiment: authors created BDNF-Cre PACAP-Cre transgenic mice used TRAP sequence genes expressed warm-sensitive neuron populations. data set RPKM normalized counts 8863 genes across 2 biological replicates (2 mice - IP Input sample ) BDNF-Cre PACAP-Cre lines. Run ptrap_de() conditions separately get two DE result tables, input ptrap_volcano2(). BDNF PACAP cells co-express BDNF PACAP genes almost set genes, including Unc3, Nxph4, Ghrh, etc. ptrap_volcano2() places results single 2D scatter plot: x-axis: logFC condition 2 y-axis: logFC condition 1 Diagonal (dotted): genes enriched equally diagonal: enriched condition 1 diagonal: enriched condition 2 Genes meet significance thresholds conditions, one, neither shown distinct colours. Scatter comparison PACAP BDNF enrichment. Genes diagonal enriched equally conditions. Genes near diagonal similarly enriched PACAP BDNF cells.
code: # Pre-computed RPKM values — already normalised; use with norm.method = "none" counts_rpkm <- read.delim(   system.file("extdata", "TAN_etal_2016_RPKM.txt", package = "pTRAPPING") )  dim(counts_rpkm) # 8863 genes × 9 columns (1 gene-ID + 8 samples) #> [1] 8863    9 names(counts_rpkm) # column names encode treatment, fraction, and replicate #> [1] "Gene"          "PACAP_Input_1" "PACAP_IP_1"    "PACAP_Input_2" #> [5] "PACAP_IP_2"    "BDNF_Input_1"  "BDNF_IP_1"     "BDNF_Input_2"  #> [9] "BDNF_IP_2" pacap_de <- counts_rpkm |>   filter(dplyr::if_any(dplyr::where(is.numeric), ~ .x > 1)) |> #keep counts > 1   mutate(gene = make.unique(Gene)) |>   column_to_rownames("gene") |>   #round() |>   ptrap_de(     test_method = "paired.ttest",     norm.method = "none",     treatment_name = "PACAP",     filter = FALSE,     lfc_threshold = 0.3,     prior.count = 0   )  # Inspect the per-gene fold enrichment pacap_de$fe #> # A tibble: 8,863 × 4 #>    Gene       FE_1  FE_2 log2_mean_FE #>    <chr>     <dbl> <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 Adcyap1    7.87  6.64         2.86 #>  2 Bdnf       4.98 10.1          2.91 #>  3 Ucn3       6.80 30.4          4.22 #>  4 Gng8       8.47 18.6          3.76 #>  5 Nme4       5.26 15.3          3.36 #>  6 Nxph4     11.0  20.6          3.98 #>  7 Hist1h2bg 13.5   1.72         2.93 #>  8 Ghrh       4.41  9.91         2.84 #>  9 Hist1h4m   5.54  4.82         2.37 #> 10 Samd3      5.44  6.43         2.57 #> # ℹ 8,853 more rows  bdnf_de <- counts_rpkm |>   filter(dplyr::if_any(dplyr::where(is.numeric), ~ .x > 1)) |> #keep counts > 1   mutate(gene = make.unique(Gene)) |>   column_to_rownames("gene") |>   #round() |>   ptrap_de(     test_method = "paired.ttest",     norm.method = "none",     treatment_name = "BDNF",     filter = FALSE,     lfc_threshold = 0.3,     prior.count = 0   )  # Inspect the per-gene fold enrichment bdnf_de$fe #> # A tibble: 8,863 × 4 #>    Gene        FE_1   FE_2 log2_mean_FE #>    <chr>      <dbl>  <dbl>        <dbl> #>  1 Adcyap1    6.16   9.05          2.93 #>  2 Bdnf       2.77   5.30          2.01 #>  3 Ucn3      14.3    7.96          3.47 #>  4 Gng8       7.38  12.7           3.33 #>  5 Nme4       9.51   4.21          2.78 #>  6 Nxph4      3.35   4.66          2.00 #>  7 Hist1h2bg  0.400 14.2           2.87 #>  8 Ghrh       6.31   5.17          2.52 #>  9 Hist1h4m  15.2    0.455         2.97 #> 10 Samd3      4.85   7.39          2.61 #> # ℹ 8,853 more rows ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1 = pacap_de$results,   de_result_2 = bdnf_de$results,   fdr = FALSE,   interactive = TRUE )

previous_headings: Step 3 — Compare two conditions with ptrap_volcano2()
title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: three DE classes named treatment labels data. Pass colors vector using names:
code: ptrap_volcano2(   pacap_de$results,   bdnf_de$results,   fdr = FALSE,   colors = c(     "DE in both" = "#D55E00",     "DE only PACAP" = "#0072B2",     "DE only BDNF" = "#009E73"   ) )

title: Getting Started with pTRAPPING
text: “identify IP/INPUT fraction column name” parser find IP INPUT keyword. Check column names use ip_level / input_level match exact spelling (e.g., input_level = "Input" mixed case). “identify replicate number column name” Every sample column must contain digit replicate number (e.g., 1, 2, 10). Add number column names supply sample_df manually. FDR column NA significant genes n = 2, multiple-testing correction extremely conservative. Try fdr = FALSE volcano functions. ≥ 4 replicates GLM method, FDR-based calls reliable. norm.method = "RPKM" requires gene.length Gene lengths (base pairs) must provided named numeric vector whose names match gene IDs count matrix. available annotation packages TxDb.* via BioMart. already pre-normalised RPKM matrix, use norm.method = "none" instead — lengths needed.

what: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: pTRAPPING takes raw count data TRAP-seq PhosphoTRAP experiment finds genes specifically enriched cell population interest — lines R. give counts table every sample column labelled treatment group, replicate number, whether IP (immunoprecipitated) INPUT (total RNA); package figures rest. Three functions cover full workflow: differential expression choice statistical engine (ptrap_de()), classic volcano plot one condition (ptrap_volcano()), paired scatter plot compare two conditions head--head (ptrap_volcano2()).

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Dependencies Bioconductor (edgeR, limma, DESeq2) installed automatically.

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: Get differential expression results interesting genes counts matrix just lines code: Plot results volcano plot annotate genes interest:  Check Getting started pTRAPPING see run full workflow, including comparing two conditions head--head making interactive volcano plots!
code: library(pTRAPPING)  # Load counts matrix counts.mat <- read.delim(   system.file("extdata", "TAN_etal_2016_raw.txt", package = "pTRAPPING") )  # Table of differentially expressed genes of interest for one condition using the default method "LRT" from edgeR ptrap_de(   counts_mat = counts.mat,   treatment_name = "PACAP",   kable.out = TRUE,   genes.filter = c(     "Adcyap1",     "Bdnf",     "Ucn3",     "Gng8",     "Fosl2",     "Junb",     "Trappc12",     "Gfap"   ) ) # Volcano plot — label a few genes of interest ptrap_de(   counts_mat = counts.mat,   treatment_name = "PACAP" ) |>   ptrap_volcano(     fdr = FALSE, # use raw p-values (n=3; FDR is very conservative)     genes.annot = c(       "Adcyap1",       "Bdnf",       "Ucn3",       "Gng8",       "Fosl2",       "Junb",       "Trappc12",       "Gfap"     )   )

title: A Suite of Functions for the Analyses of PhosphoTRAP Data in R
text: PhosphoTRAP method introduced : Knight, Z.., Tan, K., Birsoy, K., Schmidt, S., Garrison, J.L., Wysocki, R.W., Emiliano, ., Ekstrand, M.., Friedman, J.M. (2012). Molecular profiling activated neurons phosphorylated ribosome capture. Cell 151, 1126–1137. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2012.10.039 Bioconductor packages used differential expression analysis: edgeR: Robinson, M.D., McCarthy, D.J., Smyth, G.K. (2010). edgeR: Bioconductor package differential expression analysis digital gene expression data. Bioinformatics 26, 139–140. https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btp616 limma: Ritchie, M.E., Phipson, B., Wu, D., Hu, Y., Law, C.W., Shi, W., Smyth, G.K. (2015). limma powers differential expression analyses RNA-sequencing microarray studies. Nucleic Acids Res 43, e47. https://doi.org/10.1093/nar/gkv007 DESeq2: Love, M.., Huber, W., Anders, S. (2014). Moderated estimation fold change dispersion RNA-seq data DESeq2. Genome Biol 15, 550. https://doi.org/10.1186/s13059-014-0550-8 example dataset : Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.-C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-sensitive neurons control body temperature. Cell 167, 47–59. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028

what: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: PhosphoTRAP TRAP-seq experiments, RNA target cell population physically isolated immunoprecipitation — IP fraction. sample also provides INPUT: total RNA pulldown, serving background reference. ptrap_de() asks: genes enriched (depleted) IP fraction relative input? positive log2 fold change (logFC > 0) means gene's RNA abundant IP; negative logFC means less abundant. Six statistical methods available via test_method. right choice depends replicate count experimental question: "LRT" "QLF" use edgeR's generalized linear model (GLM) framework design pairs animal's IP input (~ fraction + block). apply TMM normalization (trimmed mean M values) internally, corrects differences sample composition rescaling effective library sizes rather count matrix . "QLF" conservative "LRT". norm.method applicable ignored. Recommended 4 replicates raw count data. "deseq" uses DESeq2 pipeline paired design (~ block + fraction). Normalization (median ratios) handled internally; norm.method ignored. Important: default, logFC raw maximum-likelihood estimate (MLE), can appear unrealistically large genes reads. Set shrink.lfc = TRUE pull noisy estimates towards zero — strongly recommended ranking genes producing publication figures. "voom" uses limma-voom pipeline, bridges count data linear models: converts counts log2-CPM assigns observation precision weight based mean-variance relationship, fits linear model empirical Bayes moderation. norm.method applicable ignored. "paired.ttest" runs per-gene paired t-test IP INPUT values, treating animal like matched /measurement. approach Tan et al. (2016) Cell 167, 47–59 doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 , used "RPKM" normalization; default "CPM". Recommended 3-replicate experiments, GLM-based methods struggle estimate variability reliably. logFC \(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\). "unpaired.ttest" answers different question methods: "IP enriched input?" "degree enrichment differ two treatment groups (e.g., pair-bonded vs. non-bonded animals)?" runs per-gene Welch t-test per-animal log2(IP/INPUT) fold enrichment values. Requires exactly two treatments; use treatment_name control_name specify . Count scale controlled norm.method. sample_df gene_ids optional: sample metadata can parsed automatically column names counts_mat, gene IDs first column row names.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: ptrap_de(   counts_mat,   sample_df = NULL,   gene_ids = NULL,   region_name = NULL,   treatment_name = NULL,   control_name = NULL,   sample_col = "sample",   fraction_col = "fraction",   block_col = "tube",   region_col = NULL,   treatment_col = "Treatment",   ip_level = "IP",   input_level = "INPUT",   covariates = NULL,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   test_method = c("LRT", "QLF", "paired.ttest", "unpaired.ttest", "voom", "deseq"),   norm.method = c("CPM", "RPKM", "mratios", "none"),   gene.length = NULL,   prior.count = 1,   shrink.lfc = FALSE,   return_long = FALSE,   ngenes.out = 20,   genes.filter = NULL,   kable.out = FALSE,   filter = TRUE )

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: counts_mat counts matrix one two formats: Matrix – numeric, genes x samples; column names sample IDs; gene IDs rownames supplied via gene_ids. Data frame / tibble – first column character vector gene IDs; remaining columns numeric counts sample names column names. sample_df = NULL, column names must follow naming convention described Automatic column-name parsing section. sample_df Optional data frame sample metadata. NULL (default), metadata parsed automatically column names counts_mat. provided, arguments sample_col, fraction_col, block_col, region_col, treatment_col specify columns use (falling back defaults names match). gene_ids Optional character vector gene identifiers corresponding rows counts_mat. NULL (default), gene IDs extracted first column counts_mat (data frame) rownames(counts_mat) (matrix). region_name brain region subset analyse (e.g., "POA"). region_col NULL (default) region_name supplied, function automatically scans sample_df non-structural column whose values include region_name, uses region_col exactly one column found (message shown). Can NULL data contain single region. Auto-parsing mode (sample_df = NULL): region_name additionally used column-name parsing extract region token directly column name (e.g., "POA" b1inputPOA). See Automatic column-name parsing section details. treatment_name treatment condition whose samples subsetted IP vs INPUT comparison (e.g., "pb" analyse pair-bonded samples). "unpaired.ttest", numerator condition differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), value derived automatically sample_df (parsed column names sample_df = NULL): single treatment found function proceeds silently; multiple treatments found error asks user specify one analyse. "unpaired.ttest" exactly two treatments treatment_name control_name NULL, assignments made alphabetically (first = control, second = treatment) message. control_name reference / control treatment used "unpaired.ttest". Serves denominator computing differential fold enrichment (DFE = mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control). NULL (default), function auto-detects control non-treatment_name group exactly two treatments present. sample_col Name column sample_df whose values match column names counts_mat. Ignored sample_df = NULL (auto-set internally). Default "sample". fraction_col Name column sample_df distinguishes IP INPUT fractions. Default "fraction". block_col Name column sample_df identifies IP INPUT samples come animal (blocking / pairing variable, e.g., tube ID animal number). Linking IP input control — like matched /comparison within subject — removes animal--animal variability test. GLM-based methods ("LRT", "QLF", "deseq", "voom") enters design formula; t-test methods aligns paired samples position. Default "tube". region_col Name column sample_df containing brain region labels (e.g., "BrainRegion"). NULL (default) region_name supplied, column auto-detected (see region_name). Set explicitly auto-detection ambiguous prefer explicit. Leave NULL data contain one brain region. treatment_col Name column sample_df containing treatment labels. Default "Treatment". ip_level value fraction_col identifies IP fraction. Default "IP". input_level value fraction_col identifies INPUT fraction (reference level). Default "INPUT". covariates Optional character vector covariate names include design formula. Default NULL. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed. threshold 1 means gene must least 2× (less) abundant IP vs. input. Default 1. fdr_threshold Maximum FDR (false discovery rate — expected proportion significant findings false alarms, adjusted multiple testing) allowed classify gene differentially expressed. Default 0.05. test_method Statistical method use. See @description full explanation . One : "LRT" (default) — edgeR likelihood ratio test (glmFit + glmLRT). Recommended >= 4 replicates raw count data. TMM normalisation applied internally; norm.method ignored. "QLF" — edgeR quasi-likelihood F-test (glmQLFit + glmQLFTest). conservative "LRT". TMM normalisation applied internally; norm.method ignored. "deseq" — DESeq2 pipeline (~ block + fraction). Median--ratios normalisation applied internally; norm.method ignored. logFC MLE default; use shrink.lfc = TRUE publication-ready estimates. "voom" — limma-voom pipeline (~ fraction + block) empirical Bayes moderation (lmFit + eBayes + topTable). Log2-CPM transformation precision weights applied internally; norm.method ignored. "paired.ttest" — per-gene paired t-test (IP vs. INPUT across replicates). Best n = 3 replicates. Count scale set norm.method (default "CPM"; Tan et al. 2016 used "RPKM"). P-values adjusted Benjamini-Hochberg (BH). "unpaired.ttest" — per-gene Welch t-test comparing log2(IP/INPUT) two treatment groups. Count scale set norm.method. Requires exactly two treatments (set via treatment_name control_name). norm.method Count normalisation method used t-test branches ("paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest"). Ignored "LRT", "QLF", "voom", "deseq", handle normalisation internally (message shown set methods). goal normalisation make counts comparable across samples accounting differences sequencing depth library composition. Default "CPM". One : "CPM" (default) — counts per million, scaled TMM-adjusted library sizes via edgeR::cpm(). Suitable comparing gene across replicates group. "RPKM" — CPM divided gene length kilobases, via edgeR::rpkm(). Requires gene.length. scale used Tan et al. 2016 PhosphoTRAP. Useful within-sample comparisons genes different lengths, recommended -sample comparisons. "mratios" — DESeq2 median--ratios normalisation: sample's counts divided size factor estimated geometric mean genes. directly rescales count matrix suitable -sample comparisons. Use want DESeq2-style normalisation without running full DESeq2 pipeline. "none" — normalisation; count matrix used supplied. pre-normalised inputs (e.g., TPM, FPKM) additional normalisation step redundant. gene.length Named numeric vector gene lengths base pairs (names must match gene IDs). Required norm.method = "RPKM"; ignored otherwise. Default NULL. prior.count small count added IP INPUT values computing log2 ratios (FE = (IP + prior.count) / (INPUT + prior.count)), avoid log(0). Default 1. Set 0 values already normalised guaranteed positive. Used "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". shrink.lfc Logical; applies test_method = "deseq". Raw (MLE) fold-change estimates DESeq2 can unrealistically large genes reads, making noisy rank plot. Setting shrink.lfc = TRUE corrects applying empirical Bayes shrinkage via DESeq2::lfcShrink(type = "apeglm") (requires apeglm package): estimates low-count genes pulled towards zero, giving trustworthy rankings cleaner volcano plots. P-values FDR affected — always come results(). Default FALSE, TRUE strongly recommended result used figure table. return_long Logical. used test_method "paired.ttest" "unpaired.ttest". TRUE, returns named list $results (DE tibble) $long_data (per-gene, per-animal table used test). Default FALSE. ngenes.Number top genes (sorted p-value) include output kable.= TRUE. Ignored genes.filter supplied. Default 20. genes.filter Optional character vector gene names (matching Gene column output) retain. supplied, listed genes returned ngenes.ignored. Default NULL. kable.Logical. TRUE, returns kableExtra HTML table top ngenes.genes instead full tibble. Default FALSE. filter Logical. TRUE (default), low-expression genes removed using edgeR::filterByExpr() fitting model. appropriate raw count matrices. Set filter = FALSE passing pre-normalised matrix (e.g., RPKM, TPM) via norm.method = "none", filterByExpr expects integer counts incorrectly discard genes whose normalised values fall count threshold.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: kable.= FALSE (default), tibble one row per gene sorted p-value. Columns depend test_method: "LRT" / "QLF": Gene, logFC, logCPM, LR F, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed ("", "", ""). "deseq": Gene, baseMean, logFC (MLE log2FoldChange default; empirical Bayes shrunken estimate shrink.lfc = TRUE), lfcSE, stat (Wald statistic; present MLE, absent shrink.lfc = TRUE apeglm replaces posterior estimate), PValue, FDR (Benjamini-Hochberg results(); unaffected shrinkage), treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. Genes NA p-values (low-count outliers flagged DESeq2) retained sorted bottom. "paired.ttest": always returns named list two components: $results — tibble Gene, logFC (\(\log_2(\bar{\mathrm{IP}} / \bar{\mathrm{INPUT}})\)), t_statistic, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. $fe — wide tibble Gene plus one FE_<block> column per animal, containing per-animal linear IP/INPUT fold enrichment values used test. $long_data — (return_long = TRUE) per-gene, per-animal tibble columns Gene, block column, ip_count, input_count, FE. "unpaired.ttest": Gene, logFC (log2(mean_FE_treatment / mean_FE_control)), diff_FE, mean_FE_<treatment>, mean_FE_<control>, t_statistic, df (Welch degrees freedom), PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. return_long = TRUE, returns named list: $results $long_data (per-gene, per-animal, per-treatment tibble columns Gene, block column, treatment column, FE, log2_FE). "voom": Gene, logFC, AveExpr, t, B, PValue, FDR, treatment label, optional region label, diffexpressed. kable.= TRUE, HTML kableExtra table top ngenes.genes.

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Perform differential expression analysis PhosphoTRAP data using edgeR, paired t-test, unpaired t-test, voom/limma

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: sample_df = NULL, column names counts_mat (excluding first, gene-ID column) parsed build sample metadata automatically. column name must encode three pieces information, order combination separators (_, -, ., space) separator : Treatment One letters identifying experimental group. Replicate number digit identifying biological replicate. Fraction ip_level input_level (case-insensitive); "" also accepted short alias INPUT fraction. Valid column name examples (default ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"): Region-aware parsing: region_name also supplied sample_df = NULL, parser uses region_name identify strip brain-region token column name. Matching case-insensitive extracted region stored region column auto-built sample metadata (used usual region-filtering step). Column names contain supplied region_name retain unrecognised token part treatment string; samples automatically excluded function filters treatment_name. Region-aware examples (region_name = "POA", ip_level = "IP", input_level = "INPUT"):

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
text: Tan, C.L., Cooke, E.K., Leib, D.E., Lin, Y.C., Daly, G.E., Zimmerman, C.., Knight, Z.. (2016). Warm-Sensitive Neurons Control Body Temperature. Cell 167, 47-59. doi:10.1016/j.cell.2016.08.028 Love, M.., Huber, W., Anders, S. (2014). Moderated estimation fold change dispersion RNA-seq data DESeq2. Genome Biology 15, 550. doi:10.1186/s13059-014-0550-8

title: Differential expression analysis for PhosphoTRAP data — ptrap_de
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ ## ---- Option A: simplest call -- auto-parse from column names --------------- # counts_mat is a data frame where: #   col 1      = gene IDs #   col 2+     = samples named like "b1input", "b1ip", "nb2input", etc. counts <- read.table("counts.txt", header = TRUE)  # single treatment in the matrix -- treatment_name auto-detected res <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, test_method = "paired.ttest")  # multiple treatments -- specify which one to analyze res_b <- ptrap_de(counts_mat = counts, treatment_name = "b")  ## ---- Option B: provide sample_df explicitly (original workflow) ----------- res_lrt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb" )  # quasi-likelihood F-test res_qlf <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "QLF" )  # paired t-test -- also return long-format paired table res_pt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   return_long    = TRUE ) res_pt$results    # DE tibble res_pt$long_data  # per-gene, per-animal pairs  # unpaired t-test -- compare fold enrichment between two treatments res_upt <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   control_name   = "nb",   test_method    = "unpaired.ttest" )  # voom/limma pipeline res_voom <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   region_name    = "POA",   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "voom" )  # paired t-test with CPM normalization res_cpm <- ptrap_de(   counts_mat     = counts_mat,   sample_df      = sample_df,   gene_ids       = gene_ids,   treatment_name = "pb",   test_method    = "paired.ttest",   norm.method    = "CPM" ) } # }

what: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: volcano plot puts log2 fold change (logFC, x-axis) statistical significance (-log p-value, y-axis). Genes strongly enriched statistically reliable appear upper corners — biologically meaningful candidates. Genes fail either threshold shown grey; pass coloured direction ("" enriched IP, "" depleted).

title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: ptrap_volcano(   de_result,   fdr = TRUE,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   log_base = 10,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   genes.annot = NULL,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL,   interactive = FALSE )

title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: de_result tibble returned ptrap_de(). test_method = "paired.ttest", pass $results component. fdr Logical. TRUE (default), significance assessed using FDR (false discovery rate — multiple-testing adjusted p-value); FALSE, uses raw PValue. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change gene coloured DE. value 1 means least 2× change. Default 1. fdr_threshold Significance cutoff applied column selected fdr. Default 0.05. log_base Numeric. Base logarithm p-value axis. Base 10 (default) conventional; base 2 matches Tan et al. (2016). Must positive number 1. gene_col Column name gene identifiers. Default "Gene". treatment_col Column name treatment label. Default "treatment". region_col Column name brain region label. Default "BrainRegion". colors Named vector mapping "" "" colours. Default colourblind-friendly: "#0072b2" (), "#E69f00" (). point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity highlighted points. Default 0.7. genes.annot Character vector gene names label. static mode labels drawn via ggrepel::geom_text_repel(); interactive mode omitted (use hover tooltip instead). Default NULL. max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Default 20. title Plot title. Auto-generated region treatment NULL. interactive Logical. TRUE, returns interactive plotly::ggplotly() object hover tooltips showing gene name, logFC, p-value, FDR. Gene labels (genes.annot) omitted mode. Default FALSE.

title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object, plotly object interactive = TRUE.

title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
text: Takes single tibble ptrap_de() (test_method = "paired.ttest", pass $results component). Gene labels drawn genes listed genes.annot, via ggrepel::geom_text_repel() (static mode ). DE classification recomputed inside function supplied thresholds, can explore different cutoffs without re-running ptrap_de().

title: Single volcano plot for PhosphoTRAP / TRAP-seq differential expression results — ptrap_volcano
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Default: -log10(FDR), static ptrap_volcano(res)  # Raw p-values, log2 scale -- as in Tan et al. 2016 ptrap_volcano(res, fdr = FALSE, log_base = 2)  # Interactive with hover tooltips ptrap_volcano(res, interactive = TRUE) } # }

what: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
text: run ptrap_de() two treatment groups (e.g., pair-bonded non-bonded animals) brain region, looking two separate volcano plots makes hard see genes behave similarly differently across conditions. function places results single 2D scatter: logFC (IP vs. input) condition 1 y-axis condition 2 x-axis. point one gene.

title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
code: ptrap_volcano2(   de_result_1,   de_result_2,   fdr = TRUE,   lfc_threshold = 1,   fdr_threshold = 0.05,   gene_col = "Gene",   treatment_col = "treatment",   region_col = "BrainRegion",   colors = NULL,   point_size = 3.5,   point_alpha = 0.7,   genes.annot = NULL,   max_overlaps = 20,   title = NULL,   interactive = FALSE )

title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
text: de_result_1 tibble returned ptrap_de() first treatment condition (plotted y-axis). de_result_2 tibble returned ptrap_de() second treatment condition (plotted x-axis). fdr Logical. TRUE (default), significance assessed using FDR column (BH-adjusted p-values). FALSE, raw PValue column used instead. lfc_threshold Minimum absolute log2 fold change required classify gene differentially expressed given condition (also sets vertical horizontal threshold lines). Default 1. fdr_threshold P-value cutoff used classify genes DE. Applied FDR fdr = TRUE PValue fdr = FALSE. Default 0.05. gene_col Name column containing gene identifiers result tibbles. Default "Gene". treatment_col Name column containing treatment label result tibbles. Used auto-generate axis labels DE class names. Default "treatment". region_col Name column containing brain region label. Used set default plot title. Default "BrainRegion". colors named character vector mapping DE class colour. Names must "DE ", "DE <t1>", "DE <t2>", <t1> / <t2> treatment names found data (e.g., "DE pb" "DE sol"). See examples template. NULL (default), colourblind-friendly palette used. point_size Size points. Default 3.5. point_alpha Opacity coloured (significant) points. Default 0.7. genes.annot Character vector gene names label plot via ggrepel::geom_text_repel() (static mode ). Names must match values column specified gene_col; error raised names found. Default NULL (labels). max_overlaps Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Controls many overlapping labels allowed hidden. Default 20. title Plot title. NULL (default), brain region name extracted de_result_1 used. interactive Logical. TRUE, returns interactive plotly::ggplotly() object hover tooltips showing gene name, logFC condition, p-value, FDR. Gene labels (genes.annot) omitted mode. Default FALSE.

title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
text: ggplot2::ggplot() object, plotly object interactive = TRUE.

title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
text: Reading plot: Genes near diagonal (y = x dotted line) equally enriched conditions. Genes diagonal enriched condition 1; genes enriched condition 2. Genes pass fold-change p-value thresholds conditions, one, highlighted distinct colours. Genes fail shown grey. Significance uses FDR-adjusted p-values default (fdr = TRUE); set fdr = FALSE use raw p-values instead. DE classification recomputed inside function supplied thresholds, can explore different cutoffs without re-running ptrap_de().

title: Compare IP enrichment across two treatment conditions in a single scatter plot — ptrap_volcano2
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res_pb  <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "pb") res_sol <- ptrap_de(counts_mat, sample_df, gene_ids,                     region_name = "POA", treatment_name = "sol")  # Default plot — FDR on both axes ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol)  # Use raw p-values for the DE classification ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol, fdr = FALSE)  # Interactive with hover tooltips ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol, interactive = TRUE)  # Custom thresholds, title and colours ptrap_volcano2(res_pb, res_sol,                lfc_threshold = 0.5,                fdr_threshold = 0.1,                title         = "POA: pb vs sol",                colors        = c("DE in both"  = "#D55E00",                                  "DE only pb"  = "#0072B2",                                  "DE only sol" = "#49a15f")) } # }
